TITLE: feat: Autosave protection before first manual save
LABELS: roadmap, type:feat, size:M, phase:B-robustness
---
## Context

Work done before a project has ever been saved is completely unprotected. Autosave is event-driven (there is no timer): every mutation site calls `mw.auto_save()`, but when no `current_project_file` exists yet, `auto_save()` interrupts the user with a modal "save now?" question — and if they decline (or the app crashes first), every annotation made so far is lost. `docs/11_risks_and_technical_debt.md:319-327` tracks this as the known issue "Autosave Doesn't Ask for File Location" ("New projects lose autosave protection until first save").

This issue adds a silent recovery-file mechanism: unsaved-project state is autosaved to an app-owned recovery location (known via QSettings), and on next launch the app offers to restore it.

## Current state

- `src/digitalsreeni_image_annotator/controllers/project_controller.py:548-567` — `ProjectController.auto_save()`:
  - `:549-550` — returns early while `self.mw.is_loading_project` (ADR-005; keep this).
  - `:552-563` — when `self.mw` has no `current_project_file` attribute, pops `QMessageBox.question("No Project", "You need to save the project before auto-saving…")`; on Yes it calls `save_project()`, which opens `QFileDialog.getSaveFileName` (`:391-397`). On No it returns, i.e. **nothing is saved**.
  - `:565-567` — with a project file present, calls `self.save_project(show_message=False)`.
- `auto_save()` is called from ~20 mutation sites, e.g. `controllers/annotation_controller.py:245, 434, 690, 703, 727, 795, 932`, `controllers/class_controller.py:187, 320, 355, 413, 482`, `controllers/image_controller.py:299, 918`, `controllers/dino_controller.py:315, 449`, `controllers/io_controller.py:246`. The main window delegates at `annotator_window.py:325-326`.
- `save_project()` (`project_controller.py:391-523`) is interactive: it may prompt for a path (`:392-397`) and may prompt to copy images into `<project_dir>/images/` (`:404-438`). The serializable project dict is built inline at `:440-510` and written at `:512-513`.
- `new_project()` sets `current_project_file` immediately (`project_controller.py:50`), so the unprotected window is specifically: user opens images / annotates without ever creating or saving a project.
- `annotator_window.py:384-390` — `closeEvent` only checks unsaved canvas gestures (`image_label.check_unsaved_changes()`); it does not save or warn about an unsaved project.
- QSettings precedent: `src/digitalsreeni_image_annotator/app_settings.py` (ADR-020) — `_settings()` at `:44-45` returns `QSettings("DigitalSreeni", "ImageAnnotator")`; every helper accepts an optional `settings` argument so tests can pass an INI-backed instance.

## Proposed design

1. **Extract a pure serializer.** Factor the dict-building block of `save_project()` (`project_controller.py:440-510`) into `ProjectController.build_project_data()` (no dialogs, no file I/O, no image copying). `save_project()` calls it; the recovery writer calls it too. This is the key enabler: recovery saves must never be interactive.
2. **Recovery writer.** New module `src/digitalsreeni_image_annotator/core/recovery.py` with pure-ish helpers (all accepting an optional `settings` arg, mirroring `app_settings.py`):
   - `recovery_dir()` — `QStandardPaths.writableLocation(AppDataLocation)/recovery`, created on demand.
   - `write_recovery(project_data, settings=None)` — dumps JSON to `recovery_dir()/unsaved-<timestamp>.iap.recovery` (write to a `.tmp` file then `os.replace` so a crash mid-write never leaves a truncated file), stores the path under QSettings key `recovery/pending_path`, returns the path. Reuse one stable path per app session (store it on the controller) so we overwrite, not accumulate.
   - `pending_recovery(settings=None)` — returns the stored path if the key exists **and** the file exists, else clears the key and returns None.
   - `clear_recovery(settings=None)` — deletes the file (best-effort) and removes the key.
3. **Hook into `auto_save()`.** Replace the `:552-563` prompt block: when there is no `current_project_file`, call `build_project_data()` + `recovery.write_recovery(...)` and return — no dialog, no `QFileDialog`. The `is_loading_project` early-return stays first. Skip writing when the project is trivially empty (no images, no classes, no annotations) so a fresh launch never creates a recovery file.
4. **Clear on real save.** At the end of a successful `save_project()` (after `:512-513` succeeds), call `recovery.clear_recovery()` — once the user has a real `.iap`, normal autosave takes over.
5. **Offer recovery on launch.** In `ImageAnnotator.__init__` (after the UI is built) or via a `QTimer.singleShot(0, ...)`, check `recovery.pending_recovery()`. If present, ask once: "An unsaved project from a previous session was found (last modified <mtime>). Restore it?"
   - Yes: load it through the existing `open_specific_project()` machinery *minus* the `.iap`-on-disk assumptions: set `is_loading_project = True`, `json.load` the recovery file, call `load_project_data(project_data)`, then leave `current_project_file` **unset** so the user still does their first manual save (image paths in recovery data are absolute, so images resolve).
   - No: `clear_recovery()`.
   - Either way the prompt appears at most once per launch.

**Recovery file format.** Exactly the `build_project_data()` dict serialized with `json.dump(image_utils.convert_to_serializable(...), indent=2)` — the same shape as a real `.iap` (see `project_controller.py:512-513`), so `load_project_data` consumes it with zero format-specific code. The only differences from a saved project:
- `image_paths` values are whatever absolute paths the session currently has (images were never copied into an `images/` subdir);
- the file lives outside any project dir and carries a `.iap.recovery` suffix so it can't be confused with (or double-clicked as) a real project.

## Implementation steps

1. Branch `feature/unsaved-project-recovery`.
2. Extract `build_project_data()` from `save_project()` (`project_controller.py:440-510`); `save_project()` behavior must be byte-identical for saved projects.
3. Create `core/recovery.py` with `recovery_dir` / `write_recovery` / `pending_recovery` / `clear_recovery`, all taking `settings=None` (default `QSettings("DigitalSreeni", "ImageAnnotator")` — same org/app as `app_settings._settings()` so everything lives in one store).
4. Rewrite the no-project branch of `auto_save()` (`:552-563`) to write a recovery file silently; delete the `QMessageBox.question` there.
5. Add `clear_recovery()` after successful write in `save_project()`; also call it in `new_project()` right after the first `save_project(show_message=False)` (`project_controller.py:64`) since that project is now disk-backed.
6. Add the startup restore offer in `annotator_window.py` (delegate the actual work to a new `ProjectController.offer_recovery()` to keep the orchestrator thin).
7. Guard the restore path with `is_loading_project = True` / `False` in a `try/finally`, exactly like `open_specific_project()` (`project_controller.py:118-157`).
8. Rate-limit the write if profiling shows it matters: `auto_save` fires on every mutation; for large unsaved projects a cheap monotonic-clock throttle (e.g. skip if last recovery write < 5 s ago) inside the no-project branch is acceptable — but measure first, don't add it blindly.
9. Manual test the golden path (see Test plan), then docs, senior review, PR.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Annotating with no project file no longer pops any dialog from `auto_save()`; a recovery file appears/updates under the app-data recovery dir.
- [ ] Kill the app (no save), relaunch: the restore offer appears; accepting restores images, classes (incl. keypoint schemas), and annotations; declining removes the recovery file and never asks again.
- [ ] After the first manual Ctrl+S (or New Project), the recovery file and QSettings key are cleared and never recreated while `current_project_file` exists.
- [ ] An empty session (launch, do nothing, quit) creates no recovery file.
- [ ] `save_project()` output for a normal project is unchanged (roundtrip-compatible with existing `.iap` files).
- [ ] `auto_save()` still no-ops while `is_loading_project` is True — including during recovery restore.
- [ ] No `QFileDialog` or `QMessageBox` is ever raised from inside `auto_save()`.

## Test plan

New file `tests/unit/test_recovery.py` (pattern: `tests/unit/test_app_settings.py` — INI-backed `QSettings(tmp_path/"s.ini", QSettings.Format.IniFormat)`):
- `write_recovery` → `pending_recovery` returns the path; file contains the exact dict passed (json roundtrip).
- `pending_recovery` with a stale key (file deleted) returns None and clears the key.
- `clear_recovery` removes file + key; idempotent when nothing pending.
- Atomicity: after `write_recovery`, no `*.tmp` remains in the recovery dir.
- Overwrite semantics: two `write_recovery` calls in one session produce one file, second content wins.

Extend `tests/integration/` (offscreen window, reuse `tests/conftest.py` fixtures): a test that builds a small in-memory project on the window (one class, one fake annotation), calls `mw.auto_save()` with no `current_project_file`, asserts a recovery file exists and `load_project_data(json.load(...))` restores the class/annotation on a fresh window.

Run: `pytest tests/unit/test_recovery.py tests/integration/test_smoke.py -v`

## Docs to update

- `docs/06_runtime_view.md` — add a short "Unsaved-project recovery" flow (write on mutation, offer on launch, clear on save).
- `docs/09_architecture_decisions.md` — new ADR (next free ADR number): "Silent recovery autosave for unsaved projects via QSettings-known location"; reference ADR-005 (load guard) and ADR-020 (QSettings precedent).
- `docs/11_risks_and_technical_debt.md` — resolve/remove the "Autosave Doesn't Ask for File Location" item (`:319-327`).

## Pitfalls & guardrails

- **Never prompt for a save location from the autosave path.** `auto_save()` fires from deep inside mutation handlers (annotation commit, class rename, DINO accept); a modal file dialog there re-enters the event loop mid-mutation. The recovery write must be 100% silent.
- **Always check `is_loading_project` before saving** (CLAUDE.md critical note; ADR-005) — autosave during load corrupts files. The recovery restore is itself a load: set the flag around it.
- **Do not reuse `save_project()` for recovery writes** — it prompts to copy images (`:411-438`) and mutates `image_paths` (`:522-523`). Only the extracted `build_project_data()` is safe.
- `image_paths` in recovery data are whatever absolute paths are current — fine, since restore happens on the same machine. Missing files at restore time flow through the existing `handle_missing_images` (`project_controller.py:250-268`) via `load_project_data`.
- Write via temp-file + `os.replace` — a crash during the recovery write must not destroy the previous good recovery file.
- Keep the QSettings org/app identical to `app_settings.py` (`"DigitalSreeni", "ImageAnnotator"`); pass `settings` through everywhere for testability.

## Dependencies

None

_Workflow: follow CLAUDE.md — feature branch, run smoke tests (`pytest tests/integration/test_smoke.py -v`), update arc42 docs, run the senior-reviewer agent before the PR._
